forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: df80
+project: ui-command-cleanup
+title: Card A blocker ŌĆö Move spatial focus state + nav geometry server-side so nav.* can be pure-backend plugin commands
+---
+## Why (discovered while implementing Card A 01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0)
+
+Card A asks the nine `nav.*` to become PLUGIN commands whose `execute` routes to the focus-server ops (`navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` / `set focus`). The make-or-break verification (the card's own CRITICAL VERIFICATION step) came back **client-side**: the nav ops CANNOT be driven from a plugin command with just `(window, direction)`. Two hard dependencies on React state:
+
+1. **`focused_fq` is a required wire field, not read from the kernel.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/operations.rs::Navigate` requires `focused_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker`; `DrillIn`/`DrillOut` require `fq` + `focused_fq`. The op derives the owning window from the snapshot's layer (operations.rs doc-comment: "there is no ambient window on the MCP wire... `navigate focus` derive[s] the window from the snapshot's layer"). `SpatialState` DOES hold a per-window `focus_by_window` map with `focused_in(window)` (state.rs:516), but the MCP op surface never consults it ŌĆö and even if it did, see (2).
+
+2. **`snapshot: NavSnapshot` is live DOM geometry, only knowable in the webview.** `state.rs::navigate` / `focus` run `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` over the rects of every sibling scope. The snapshot is built React-side by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` ŌåÆ `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot(layerFq)`, which calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on each mounted scope's DOM node AT CALL TIME (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/layer-scope-registry-context.tsx`). The Rust kernel has no DOM and cannot synthesize this.
+
+So a plugin command's `execute` (which runs in the host, not the webview) cannot supply `focused_fq` + `snapshot`. The current React closures (`buildNavCommands` / `buildDrillCommands` in app-shell.tsx) work precisely because they read live `actions.focusedFq()` and build the snapshot from the live registry, then call `lib/focus-mcp.ts::navigateFocus/drillIn/drillOut`.
+
+## What this card must do (one viable design, to be confirmed by owner)
+
+Make the focus kernel authoritative for BOTH current focus AND scope geometry per window, so a `(window, direction)` call is sufficient:
+- The kernel already tracks `focus_by_window`. Add a per-window **registered-scope geometry** store on the kernel (push/update rects via the existing layer/scope registration path) so the kernel can build its own `NavSnapshot` for the focused window without a React-supplied snapshot.
+- Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to accept just `{ window, direction }` (or `{ window }`), resolving `focused_fq` from `focus_by_window[window]` and the snapshot from the kernel-held geometry.
+- Keep the React side feeding geometry to the kernel on mount/resize/scroll (it already pushes layers/scopes; this extends that to rects), instead of building a snapshot per keystroke.
+- Then Card A can wire the nav plugin `execute` straight to the ops.
+
+This is a substantial focus-kernel change and is OUT OF SCOPE for Card A. Card A is blocked on it for the pure-backend wiring.
+
+## Acceptance
+- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` operate on the kernel's per-window current focus + kernel-held geometry; a caller passes only `{ window, direction }` (no `focused_fq`, no `snapshot`).
+- [ ] React feeds scope geometry to the kernel through the existing registration path (no per-keystroke snapshot build).
+- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.
+
+#bug
\ No newline at end of file

-

-
-1. **`focused_fq` is a required wire field, not read from the kernel.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/operations.rs::Navigate` requires `focused_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker`; `DrillIn`/`DrillOut` require `fq` + `focused_fq`. The snapshot is built React-side by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` ŌåÆ `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot(layerFq)`, which calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on each mounted scope's DOM node AT CALL TIME (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/layer-scope-registry-context.tsx`). The Rust kernel has no DOM and cannot synthesize this.
-
-So a plugin command's `execute` (which runs in the host, not the webview) cannot supply `focused_fq` + `snapshot`. The current React closures (`buildNavCommands` / `buildDrillCommands` in app-shell.tsx) work precisely because they read live `actions.focusedFq()` and build the snapshot from the live registry, then call `lib/focus-mcp.ts::navigateFocus/drillIn/drillOut`.
-
-## What this card must do (one viable design, to be confirmed by owner)
-
-Make the focus kernel authoritative for BOTH current focus AND scope geometry per window, so a `(window, direction)` call is sufficient:
-- The kernel already tracks `focus_by_window`. Add a per-window **registered-scope geometry** store on the kernel (push/update rects via the existing layer/scope registration path) so the kernel can build its own `NavSnapshot` for the focused window without a React-supplied snapshot.

-

-- [ ] React feeds scope geometry to the kernel through the existing registration path (no per-keystroke snapshot build).
-- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.

\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -2,31 +2,35 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: df80
 project: ui-command-cleanup
-title: Card A blocker ŌĆö Move spatial focus state + nav geometry server-side so nav.* can be pure-backend plugin commands
+title: Card F2 ŌĆö Extend the focus service with on-demand UI-geometry queries (scope chain, focus, geometry)
 ---
-## Why (discovered while implementing Card A 01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0)
+## What
+Owner decision: there is a SERVICE about UI geometry ŌĆö **scope chain, focus, and geometry** ŌĆö and it lives by EXTENDING the existing focus service/kernel (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus`), which already owns per-window focus and runs `pick_target` host-side over a `NavSnapshot`. Plugins query it ON DEMAND (not continuous sync) to get the current UI geometry, so geometry-dependent commands (nav.*, jump, ŌĆ”) can be real plugin commands triggered from ANY source (OS menu, keystroke, agent).

-Card A asks the nine `nav.*` to become PLUGIN commands whose `execute` routes to the focus-server ops (`navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` / `set focus`). The make-or-break verification (the card's own CRITICAL VERIFICATION step) came back **client-side**: the nav ops CANNOT be driven from a plugin command with just `(window, direction)`. Two hard dependencies on React state:
+Built on Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel). Pull, per window, on demand from the webview:
+- **scope chain** (the focused scope chain the UI currently sends to the backend),
+- **focus** (the focused FullyQualifiedMoniker),
+- **geometry** (the `NavSnapshot`: per-scope `getBoundingClientRect` rects), built by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` / `layer-scope-registry-context.tsx::buildSnapshot`.

-1. **`focused_fq` is a required wire field, not read from the kernel.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/operations.rs::Navigate` requires `focused_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker`; `DrillIn`/`DrillOut` require `fq` + `focused_fq`. The op derives the owning window from the snapshot's layer (operations.rs doc-comment: "there is no ambient window on the MCP wire... `navigate focus` derive[s] the window from the snapshot's layer"). `SpatialState` DOES hold a per-window `focus_by_window` map with `focused_in(window)` (state.rs:516), but the MCP op surface never consults it ŌĆö and even if it did, see (2).
+### Work
+- Add focus-service ops (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/{server.rs,operations.rs,state.rs}`) that, given a window, fetch the current scope chain / focus / snapshot by issuing an F1 `request_from_ui(window, "focus.geometry"/"focus.scopeChain"/"focus.current", ŌĆ”)` and returning the result. Expose them on the focus module so plugins call `this.focus.<op>()` (the `focus` module is already plugin-reachable via `command_services.rs::install_app_command_services`).
+- UI responder: register (via F1 `registerUiResponder`) handlers in `spatial-focus-context.tsx` that answer these by building the snapshot / reading the current focus + scope chain.
+- `pick_target` and focus mutation STAY host-side in the kernel (`state.rs::navigate`/`focus`) ŌĆö they already consume a `NavSnapshot`. The new query ops just supply that snapshot on demand instead of requiring the caller (React) to pass it.

-2. **`snapshot: NavSnapshot` is live DOM geometry, only knowable in the webview.** `state.rs::navigate` / `focus` run `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` over the rects of every sibling scope. The snapshot is built React-side by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` ŌåÆ `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot(layerFq)`, which calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on each mounted scope's DOM node AT CALL TIME (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/layer-scope-registry-context.tsx`). The Rust kernel has no DOM and cannot synthesize this.
+### Why this unblocks nav.* (Card A)
+With on-demand geometry, a `nav.*` plugin command's `execute` can: pull the snapshot+focus from the focus service, run the existing `navigate`/`drill_in`/`drill_out` kernel logic, and apply focus ŌĆö entirely host-side, no live React handle. nav becomes a real plugin command that works from the OS menu.

-So a plugin command's `execute` (which runs in the host, not the webview) cannot supply `focused_fq` + `snapshot`. The current React closures (`buildNavCommands` / `buildDrillCommands` in app-shell.tsx) work precisely because they read live `actions.focusedFq()` and build the snapshot from the live registry, then call `lib/focus-mcp.ts::navigateFocus/drillIn/drillOut`.
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A plugin (HostInternal caller) can call the focus service to get the current scope chain, focus FQM, and geometry NavSnapshot for a window, pulled on demand from the webview via F1.
+- [ ] A host-side navigation computed from a pulled snapshot returns the correct target (reuses `pick_target`), with no caller-supplied snapshot needed.
+- [ ] No new continuous-sync path is introduced; geometry is pulled per request.

-## What this card must do (one viable design, to be confirmed by owner)
+## Tests
+- [ ] Rust integration test: a fake UI responder returns a known NavSnapshot/scope-chain/focus; assert the focus service query ops return them, and that a navigate over the pulled snapshot yields the expected target. Covers all three queries.
+- [ ] Tests fail before the ops exist (RED), pass after.

-Make the focus kernel authoritative for BOTH current focus AND scope geometry per window, so a `(window, direction)` call is sufficient:
-- The kernel already tracks `focus_by_window`. Add a per-window **registered-scope geometry** store on the kernel (push/update rects via the existing layer/scope registration path) so the kernel can build its own `NavSnapshot` for the focused window without a React-supplied snapshot.
-- Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to accept just `{ window, direction }` (or `{ window }`), resolving `focused_fq` from `focus_by_window[window]` and the snapshot from the kernel-held geometry.
-- Keep the React side feeding geometry to the kernel on mount/resize/scroll (it already pushes layers/scopes; this extends that to rects), instead of building a snapshot per keystroke.
-- Then Card A can wire the nav plugin `execute` straight to the ops.
-
-This is a substantial focus-kernel change and is OUT OF SCOPE for Card A. Card A is blocked on it for the pure-backend wiring.
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö failing test first. Automated tests only.

-## Acceptance
-- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` operate on the kernel's per-window current focus + kernel-held geometry; a caller passes only `{ window, direction }` (no `focused_fq`, no `snapshot`).
-- [ ] React feeds scope geometry to the kernel through the existing registration path (no per-keystroke snapshot build).
-- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.
-
-#bug
\ No newline at end of file
+## Depends on
+- Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel).
\ No newline at end of file

 ---
-## What
-Owner decision: there is a SERVICE about UI geometry ŌĆö **scope chain, focus, and geometry** ŌĆö and it lives by EXTENDING the existing focus service/kernel (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus`), which already owns per-window focus and runs `pick_target` host-side over a `NavSnapshot`. Plugins query it ON DEMAND (not continuous sync) to get the current UI geometry, so geometry-dependent commands (nav.*, jump, ŌĆ”) can be real plugin commands triggered from ANY source (OS menu, keystroke, agent).
+## Why (discovered while implementing Card A 01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0)

-Built on Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel). Pull, per window, on demand from the webview:
-- **scope chain** (the focused scope chain the UI currently sends to the backend),
-- **focus** (the focused FullyQualifiedMoniker),
-- **geometry** (the `NavSnapshot`: per-scope `getBoundingClientRect` rects), built by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` / `layer-scope-registry-context.tsx::buildSnapshot`.
+Card A asks the nine `nav.*` to become PLUGIN commands whose `execute` routes to the focus-server ops (`navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` / `set focus`). The make-or-break verification (the card's own CRITICAL VERIFICATION step) came back **client-side**: the nav ops CANNOT be driven from a plugin command with just `(window, direction)`. Two hard dependencies on React state:
+
+1. **`focused_fq` is a required wire field, not read from the kernel.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/operations.rs::Navigate` requires `focused_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker`; `DrillIn`/`DrillOut` require `fq` + `focused_fq`. The op derives the owning window from the snapshot's layer (operations.rs doc-comment: "there is no ambient window on the MCP wire... `navigate focus` derive[s] the window from the snapshot's layer"). `SpatialState` DOES hold a per-window `focus_by_window` map with `focused_in(window)` (state.rs:516), but the MCP op surface never consults it ŌĆö and even if it did, see (2).
+
+2. **`snapshot: NavSnapshot` is live DOM geometry, only knowable in the webview.** `state.rs::navigate` / `focus` run `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` over the rects of every sibling scope. The snapshot is built React-side by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` ŌåÆ `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot(layerFq)`, which calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on each mounted scope's DOM node AT CALL TIME (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/layer-scope-registry-context.tsx`). The Rust kernel has no DOM and cannot synthesize this.

-### Work
-- Add focus-service ops (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/{server.rs,operations.rs,state.rs}`) that, given a window, fetch the current scope chain / focus / snapshot by issuing an F1 `request_from_ui(window, "focus.geometry"/"focus.scopeChain"/"focus.current", ŌĆ”)` and returning the result. Expose them on the focus module so plugins call `this.focus.<op>()` (the `focus` module is already plugin-reachable via `command_services.rs::install_app_command_services`).
-- UI responder: register (via F1 `registerUiResponder`) handlers in `spatial-focus-context.tsx` that answer these by building the snapshot / reading the current focus + scope chain.
-- `pick_target` and focus mutation STAY host-side in the kernel (`state.rs::navigate`/`focus`) ŌĆö they already consume a `NavSnapshot`. The new query ops just supply that snapshot on demand instead of requiring the caller (React) to pass it.
+So a plugin command's `execute` (which runs in the host, not the webview) cannot supply `focused_fq` + `snapshot`. The current React closures (`buildNavCommands` / `buildDrillCommands` in app-shell.tsx) work precisely because they read live `actions.focusedFq()` and build the snapshot from the live registry, then call `lib/focus-mcp.ts::navigateFocus/drillIn/drillOut`.

-### Why this unblocks nav.* (Card A)
-With on-demand geometry, a `nav.*` plugin command's `execute` can: pull the snapshot+focus from the focus service, run the existing `navigate`/`drill_in`/`drill_out` kernel logic, and apply focus ŌĆö entirely host-side, no live React handle. nav becomes a real plugin command that works from the OS menu.
+## What this card must do (one viable design, to be confirmed by owner)

-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A plugin (HostInternal caller) can call the focus service to get the current scope chain, focus FQM, and geometry NavSnapshot for a window, pulled on demand from the webview via F1.
-- [ ] A host-side navigation computed from a pulled snapshot returns the correct target (reuses `pick_target`), with no caller-supplied snapshot needed.
-- [ ] No new continuous-sync path is introduced; geometry is pulled per request.
+Make the focus kernel authoritative for BOTH current focus AND scope geometry per window, so a `(window, direction)` call is sufficient:
+- The kernel already tracks `focus_by_window`. Add a per-window **registered-scope geometry** store on the kernel (push/update rects via the existing layer/scope registration path) so the kernel can build its own `NavSnapshot` for the focused window without a React-supplied snapshot.
+- Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to accept just `{ window, direction }` (or `{ window }`), resolving `focused_fq` from `focus_by_window[window]` and the snapshot from the kernel-held geometry.
+- Keep the React side feeding geometry to the kernel on mount/resize/scroll (it already pushes layers/scopes; this extends that to rects), instead of building a snapshot per keystroke.
+- Then Card A can wire the nav plugin `execute` straight to the ops.

-## Tests
-- [ ] Rust integration test: a fake UI responder returns a known NavSnapshot/scope-chain/focus; assert the focus service query ops return them, and that a navigate over the pulled snapshot yields the expected target. Covers all three queries.
-- [ ] Tests fail before the ops exist (RED), pass after.
+This is a substantial focus-kernel change and is OUT OF SCOPE for Card A. Card A is blocked on it for the pure-backend wiring.

-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö failing test first. Automated tests only.
+## Acceptance
+- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` operate on the kernel's per-window current focus + kernel-held geometry; a caller passes only `{ window, direction }` (no `focused_fq`, no `snapshot`).
+- [ ] React feeds scope geometry to the kernel through the existing registration path (no per-keystroke snapshot build).
+- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.

-## Depends on
-- Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel).

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,35 +4,36 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: df80
 project: ui-command-cleanup
-title: Card F2 ŌĆö Extend the focus service with on-demand UI-geometry queries (scope chain, focus, geometry)
+title: Card A blocker ŌĆö Make the focus kernel authoritative for current focus (drop focused_fq wire field); geometry STAYS computed on-demand
 ---
-## What
-Owner decision: there is a SERVICE about UI geometry ŌĆö **scope chain, focus, and geometry** ŌĆö and it lives by EXTENDING the existing focus service/kernel (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus`), which already owns per-window focus and runs `pick_target` host-side over a `NavSnapshot`. Plugins query it ON DEMAND (not continuous sync) to get the current UI geometry, so geometry-dependent commands (nav.*, jump, ŌĆ”) can be real plugin commands triggered from ANY source (OS menu, keystroke, agent).
+## Ōøö OWNER CONSTRAINT + DESIGN CORRECTION (2026-06-06, authoritative ŌĆö supersedes the "What this card must do" section below)
+Owner ruling: **geometry MUST be computed on demand. No caching, no partial/incremental build, no kernel-held geometry store, no push-on-mount/resize/scroll.** Direct quote: *"every time we've tried anything other than compute the geometry on demand ŌĆö any kind of caching or partial build, you have failed to do it correctly."* The per-keystroke `getBoundingClientRect()` on-demand snapshot build in the webview **STAYS**.

-Built on Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel). Pull, per window, on demand from the webview:
-- **scope chain** (the focused scope chain the UI currently sends to the backend),
-- **focus** (the focused FullyQualifiedMoniker),
-- **geometry** (the `NavSnapshot`: per-scope `getBoundingClientRect` rects), built by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` / `layer-scope-registry-context.tsx::buildSnapshot`.
+This INVALIDATES the original proposal's central mechanism (a per-window kernel geometry store fed by React on mount/resize/scroll). Do NOT build that.

-### Work
-- Add focus-service ops (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/{server.rs,operations.rs,state.rs}`) that, given a window, fetch the current scope chain / focus / snapshot by issuing an F1 `request_from_ui(window, "focus.geometry"/"focus.scopeChain"/"focus.current", ŌĆ”)` and returning the result. Expose them on the focus module so plugins call `this.focus.<op>()` (the `focus` module is already plugin-reachable via `command_services.rs::install_app_command_services`).
-- UI responder: register (via F1 `registerUiResponder`) handlers in `spatial-focus-context.tsx` that answer these by building the snapshot / reading the current focus + scope chain.
-- `pick_target` and focus mutation STAY host-side in the kernel (`state.rs::navigate`/`focus`) ŌĆö they already consume a `NavSnapshot`. The new query ops just supply that snapshot on demand instead of requiring the caller (React) to pass it.
+### Corrected scope (do this)
+1. **Focus identity ŌåÆ server-side.** The kernel already holds `focus_by_window: HashMap<WindowLabel, FullyQualifiedMoniker>` (`state.rs:198`). Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to **drop the required `focused_fq` wire field** (`operations.rs:105/206/228`) and resolve current focus from `focus_by_window[window]` instead.
+2. **Geometry ŌåÆ unchanged, on-demand.** `snapshot: NavSnapshot` remains a per-call input, freshly built in the webview at dispatch time from live DOM rects. The kernel does NOT hold or cache geometry.
+3. **Op shape becomes `{ window, direction, snapshot }`** (snapshot still required & fresh; `focused_fq` removed). Same for drill in/out.
+4. **nav.* are handler-bus plugin commands**, NOT pure-backend: their Card B (`01KTED5FŌĆ”`) webview handler builds the on-demand snapshot and calls the op; the kernel supplies `focused_fq`. ("Pure-backend `{window, direction}` only" is impossible under the on-demand-geometry constraint and is explicitly dropped.)

-### Why this unblocks nav.* (Card A)
-With on-demand geometry, a `nav.*` plugin command's `execute` can: pull the snapshot+focus from the focus service, run the existing `navigate`/`drill_in`/`drill_out` kernel logic, and apply focus ŌĆö entirely host-side, no live React handle. nav becomes a real plugin command that works from the OS menu.
+### Revised Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` no longer require `focused_fq` on the wire ŌĆö the kernel resolves current focus from `focus_by_window[window]`.
+- [ ] `snapshot` is still supplied fresh per call, built on-demand from live DOM rects in the webview ŌĆö NO kernel-held geometry, NO cache, NO push model.
+- [ ] `nav.*` are plugin commands whose webview handler builds the on-demand snapshot and calls the op; Card A wires them via the Card B handler bus.
+- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.
+
+---
+## (ORIGINAL PROPOSAL ŌĆö kept for context; its kernel-geometry-store mechanism is SUPERSEDED by the banner above)
+
+## Why (discovered while implementing Card A 01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0)
+
+Card A asks the nine `nav.*` to become PLUGIN commands whose `execute` routes to the focus-server ops (`navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` / `set focus`). The make-or-break verification came back **client-side**: the nav ops CANNOT be driven from a plugin command with just `(window, direction)`. Two hard dependencies on React state:

-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A plugin (HostInternal caller) can call the focus service to get the current scope chain, focus FQM, and geometry NavSnapshot for a window, pulled on demand from the webview via F1.
-- [ ] A host-side navigation computed from a pulled snapshot returns the correct target (reuses `pick_target`), with no caller-supplied snapshot needed.
-- [ ] No new continuous-sync path is introduced; geometry is pulled per request.
+1. **`focused_fq` is a required wire field, not read from the kernel.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/operations.rs::Navigate` requires `focused_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker`; `DrillIn`/`DrillOut` require `fq` + `focused_fq`. `SpatialState` DOES hold a per-window `focus_by_window` map with `focused_in(window)` (state.rs:516), but the MCP op surface never consults it.

-## Tests
-- [ ] Rust integration test: a fake UI responder returns a known NavSnapshot/scope-chain/focus; assert the focus service query ops return them, and that a navigate over the pulled snapshot yields the expected target. Covers all three queries.
-- [ ] Tests fail before the ops exist (RED), pass after.
+2. **`snapshot: NavSnapshot` is live DOM geometry, only knowable in the webview.** `state.rs::navigate` / `focus` run `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` over the rects of every sibling scope. The snapshot is built React-side by `spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` ŌåÆ `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot(layerFq)`, which calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on each mounted scope's DOM node AT CALL TIME. The Rust kernel has no DOM.

-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö failing test first. Automated tests only.
+So a plugin command's `execute` (host, not webview) cannot supply `focused_fq` + `snapshot`. (Finding #1 is fixed by the corrected scope above; finding #2 is WHY geometry stays on-demand in the webview.)

-## Depends on
-- Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel).
\ No newline at end of file
+#bug
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original

@@ -4,36 +4,35 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: df80
 project: ui-command-cleanup
-title: Card A blocker ŌĆö Make the focus kernel authoritative for current focus (drop focused_fq wire field); geometry STAYS computed on-demand
+title: Card F2 ŌĆö Extend the focus service with on-demand UI-geometry queries (scope chain, focus, geometry)
 ---
-## Ōøö OWNER CONSTRAINT + DESIGN CORRECTION (2026-06-06, authoritative ŌĆö supersedes the "What this card must do" section below)
-Owner ruling: **geometry MUST be computed on demand. No caching, no partial/incremental build, no kernel-held geometry store, no push-on-mount/resize/scroll.** Direct quote: *"every time we've tried anything other than compute the geometry on demand ŌĆö any kind of caching or partial build, you have failed to do it correctly."* The per-keystroke `getBoundingClientRect()` on-demand snapshot build in the webview **STAYS**.
-
-This INVALIDATES the original proposal's central mechanism (a per-window kernel geometry store fed by React on mount/resize/scroll). Do NOT build that.
-
-### Corrected scope (do this)
-1. **Focus identity ŌåÆ server-side.** The kernel already holds `focus_by_window: HashMap<WindowLabel, FullyQualifiedMoniker>` (`state.rs:198`). Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to **drop the required `focused_fq` wire field** (`operations.rs:105/206/228`) and resolve current focus from `focus_by_window[window]` instead.
-2. **Geometry ŌåÆ unchanged, on-demand.** `snapshot: NavSnapshot` remains a per-call input, freshly built in the webview at dispatch time from live DOM rects. The kernel does NOT hold or cache geometry.
-3. **Op shape becomes `{ window, direction, snapshot }`** (snapshot still required & fresh; `focused_fq` removed). Same for drill in/out.
-4. **nav.* are handler-bus plugin commands**, NOT pure-backend: their Card B (`01KTED5FŌĆ”`) webview handler builds the on-demand snapshot and calls the op; the kernel supplies `focused_fq`. ("Pure-backend `{window, direction}` only" is impossible under the on-demand-geometry constraint and is explicitly dropped.)
-
-### Revised Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` no longer require `focused_fq` on the wire ŌĆö the kernel resolves current focus from `focus_by_window[window]`.
-- [ ] `snapshot` is still supplied fresh per call, built on-demand from live DOM rects in the webview ŌĆö NO kernel-held geometry, NO cache, NO push model.
-- [ ] `nav.*` are plugin commands whose webview handler builds the on-demand snapshot and calls the op; Card A wires them via the Card B handler bus.
-- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.
+## What
+Owner decision: there is a SERVICE about UI geometry ŌĆö **scope chain, focus, and geometry** ŌĆö and it lives by EXTENDING the existing focus service/kernel (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus`), which already owns per-window focus and runs `pick_target` host-side over a `NavSnapshot`. Plugins query it ON DEMAND (not continuous sync) to get the current UI geometry, so geometry-dependent commands (nav.*, jump, ŌĆ”) can be real plugin commands triggered from ANY source (OS menu, keystroke, agent).

----
-## (ORIGINAL PROPOSAL ŌĆö kept for context; its kernel-geometry-store mechanism is SUPERSEDED by the banner above)
+Built on Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel). Pull, per window, on demand from the webview:
+- **scope chain** (the focused scope chain the UI currently sends to the backend),
+- **focus** (the focused FullyQualifiedMoniker),
+- **geometry** (the `NavSnapshot`: per-scope `getBoundingClientRect` rects), built by `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` / `layer-scope-registry-context.tsx::buildSnapshot`.

-## Why (discovered while implementing Card A 01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0)
+### Work
+- Add focus-service ops (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/{server.rs,operations.rs,state.rs}`) that, given a window, fetch the current scope chain / focus / snapshot by issuing an F1 `request_from_ui(window, "focus.geometry"/"focus.scopeChain"/"focus.current", ŌĆ”)` and returning the result. Expose them on the focus module so plugins call `this.focus.<op>()` (the `focus` module is already plugin-reachable via `command_services.rs::install_app_command_services`).
+- UI responder: register (via F1 `registerUiResponder`) handlers in `spatial-focus-context.tsx` that answer these by building the snapshot / reading the current focus + scope chain.
+- `pick_target` and focus mutation STAY host-side in the kernel (`state.rs::navigate`/`focus`) ŌĆö they already consume a `NavSnapshot`. The new query ops just supply that snapshot on demand instead of requiring the caller (React) to pass it.

-Card A asks the nine `nav.*` to become PLUGIN commands whose `execute` routes to the focus-server ops (`navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` / `set focus`). The make-or-break verification came back **client-side**: the nav ops CANNOT be driven from a plugin command with just `(window, direction)`. Two hard dependencies on React state:
+### Why this unblocks nav.* (Card A)
+With on-demand geometry, a `nav.*` plugin command's `execute` can: pull the snapshot+focus from the focus service, run the existing `navigate`/`drill_in`/`drill_out` kernel logic, and apply focus ŌĆö entirely host-side, no live React handle. nav becomes a real plugin command that works from the OS menu.

-1. **`focused_fq` is a required wire field, not read from the kernel.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/operations.rs::Navigate` requires `focused_fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker`; `DrillIn`/`DrillOut` require `fq` + `focused_fq`. `SpatialState` DOES hold a per-window `focus_by_window` map with `focused_in(window)` (state.rs:516), but the MCP op surface never consults it.
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A plugin (HostInternal caller) can call the focus service to get the current scope chain, focus FQM, and geometry NavSnapshot for a window, pulled on demand from the webview via F1.
+- [ ] A host-side navigation computed from a pulled snapshot returns the correct target (reuses `pick_target`), with no caller-supplied snapshot needed.
+- [ ] No new continuous-sync path is introduced; geometry is pulled per request.

-2. **`snapshot: NavSnapshot` is live DOM geometry, only knowable in the webview.** `state.rs::navigate` / `focus` run `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` over the rects of every sibling scope. The snapshot is built React-side by `spatial-focus-context.tsx::buildSnapshotForFocused` ŌåÆ `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot(layerFq)`, which calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on each mounted scope's DOM node AT CALL TIME. The Rust kernel has no DOM.
+## Tests
+- [ ] Rust integration test: a fake UI responder returns a known NavSnapshot/scope-chain/focus; assert the focus service query ops return them, and that a navigate over the pulled snapshot yields the expected target. Covers all three queries.
+- [ ] Tests fail before the ops exist (RED), pass after.

-So a plugin command's `execute` (host, not webview) cannot supply `focused_fq` + `snapshot`. (Finding #1 is fixed by the corrected scope above; finding #2 is WHY geometry stays on-demand in the webview.)
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö failing test first. Automated tests only.

-#bug
\ No newline at end of file
+## Depends on
+- Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel).
\ No newline at end of file

+The reconciliation of "nav.* server-side" + "geometry on-demand": the nav command runs **host-side**, and obtains geometry by **requesting a fresh snapshot from the webview over the F1 channel and awaiting it** ŌĆö never from a cache.
+
+1. **Focus identity ŌåÆ server-side.** The kernel already holds `focus_by_window: HashMap<WindowLabel, FullyQualifiedMoniker>` (`state.rs:198`). Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to **drop the required `focused_fq` wire field** (`operations.rs:105/206/228`) and resolve current focus from `focus_by_window[window]`.
+2. **Geometry ŌåÆ on-demand via F1, not cached, not webview-local.** When a nav op needs the `NavSnapshot`, the host issues an F1 `request_from_ui(window, "focus.geometry", {layer/focused})` and awaits the reply; the webview's F1 responder builds the snapshot ON DEMAND (`getBoundingClientRect()` at that instant via `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot`) and replies. The kernel runs `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` on that fresh reply. Kernel holds NO geometry.
+3. **nav.* are host-driven plugin commands** (NOT Card B webview handlers): their `execute` runs host-side, resolves `focused_fq` from the kernel, fetches the on-demand snapshot via F1, calls the op. Card A then wires the nav plugin `execute` straight to this path.
+4. **Lock discipline (inherited from F1):** the host must DROP all `AppState`/spatial locks before awaiting the F1 reply, then re-acquire ŌĆö see F1's deadlock rule.

-### Revised Acceptance Criteria
+## Acceptance Criteria

+- [ ] nav.* are host-driven plugin commands whose `execute` resolves focus from the kernel + fetches geometry via F1; Card A wires them to this path (pure-backend command identity, geometry round-trips to the UI on demand).
+- [ ] No `AppState`/spatial lock is held across the F1 await (deadlock-safe).
 - [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.

+## Depends on
+- `01KTEFP1RMM2G65GR1PH9YQWEZ` ŌĆö Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel). This card rides on it.
+
 ---
-## (ORIGINAL PROPOSAL ŌĆö kept for context; its kernel-geometry-store mechanism is SUPERSEDED by the banner above)

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,26 +11,32 @@

 This INVALIDATES the original proposal's kernel-geometry-store mechanism (a per-window kernel store fed by React on mount/resize/scroll). Do NOT build that.

+## ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ CAUSES A NAV REGRESSION ŌĆö see `01KTESYQ49JYJB2YT1WXYKK0W4`
+The implementation of this card (host-driven nav, kernel-authoritative focus) **broke arrow-key navigation** in the working tree. AC #1 below ("kernel resolves current focus from `focus_by_window`") is the prime suspect: the frontend is the real source of truth for focus (`FRONTEND_AUTHORITATIVE_KINDS = { scope_chain }`), so `focus_by_window` goes stale (esp. on click-to-focus) and the nav op drops. The regression card proposes resolving `from` from the webview's `focus.current` (the provider already exposes it) instead of the kernel map. Reconcile that against AC #1 before marking this done. The geometry pull + lock discipline are correct and not implicated.
+
 ## Design (re-aligned 2026-06-06 to the F1/F2 approach)
 This card is the **"Card F2 ŌĆö focus geometry queries"** that **Card F1 `01KTEFP1RMM2G65GR1PH9YQWEZ`** (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel) is built for. It depends on F1.

 The reconciliation of "nav.* server-side" + "geometry on-demand": the nav command runs **host-side**, and obtains geometry by **requesting a fresh snapshot from the webview over the F1 channel and awaiting it** ŌĆö never from a cache.

-1. **Focus identity ŌåÆ server-side.** The kernel already holds `focus_by_window: HashMap<WindowLabel, FullyQualifiedMoniker>` (`state.rs:198`). Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to **drop the required `focused_fq` wire field** (`operations.rs:105/206/228`) and resolve current focus from `focus_by_window[window]`.
+1. **Focus identity ŌåÆ server-side.** The kernel already holds `focus_by_window: HashMap<WindowLabel, FullyQualifiedMoniker>` (`state.rs:198`). Change `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` to **drop the required `focused_fq` wire field** (`operations.rs:105/206/228`) and resolve current focus from `focus_by_window[window]`. **ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ See the regression note above ŌĆö this exact choice is implicated; resolving from the webview's `focus.current` may be required instead.**
 2. **Geometry ŌåÆ on-demand via F1, not cached, not webview-local.** When a nav op needs the `NavSnapshot`, the host issues an F1 `request_from_ui(window, "focus.geometry", {layer/focused})` and awaits the reply; the webview's F1 responder builds the snapshot ON DEMAND (`getBoundingClientRect()` at that instant via `LayerScopeRegistry.buildSnapshot`) and replies. The kernel runs `IndexedSnapshot::new(snapshot)` + `pick_target` on that fresh reply. Kernel holds NO geometry.
 3. **nav.* are host-driven plugin commands** (NOT Card B webview handlers): their `execute` runs host-side, resolves `focused_fq` from the kernel, fetches the on-demand snapshot via F1, calls the op. Card A then wires the nav plugin `execute` straight to this path.
 4. **Lock discipline (inherited from F1):** the host must DROP all `AppState`/spatial locks before awaiting the F1 reply, then re-acquire ŌĆö see F1's deadlock rule.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` no longer require `focused_fq` on the wire ŌĆö the kernel resolves current focus from `focus_by_window[window]`.
+- [ ] `navigate focus` / `drill_in layer` / `drill_out layer` no longer require `focused_fq` on the wire ŌĆö current focus resolved server-side. **(Reconcile kernel `focus_by_window` vs webview `focus.current` per the regression card before closing.)**
 - [ ] The nav op's `NavSnapshot` is obtained per call via the F1 hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel, built on-demand in the webview (`getBoundingClientRect`) ŌĆö NO kernel-held geometry, NO cache, NO push model, NO webview-local handler owning the nav logic.
-- [ ] nav.* are host-driven plugin commands whose `execute` resolves focus from the kernel + fetches geometry via F1; Card A wires them to this path (pure-backend command identity, geometry round-trips to the UI on demand).
+- [ ] nav.* are host-driven plugin commands whose `execute` resolves focus + fetches geometry via F1; Card A wires them to this path.
 - [ ] No `AppState`/spatial lock is held across the F1 await (deadlock-safe).
-- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green.
+- [ ] Existing spatial-nav behavior (beam search, drill, focus-lost fallback) preserved ŌĆö full spatial test suite green, AND a real-path (non-fake-provider) nav test passes (see regression card).

 ## Depends on
 - `01KTEFP1RMM2G65GR1PH9YQWEZ` ŌĆö Card F1 (hostŌåÆUI request/reply channel). This card rides on it.

+## Quality gate
+- `01KTESYQ49JYJB2YT1WXYKK0W4` ŌĆö host-driven nav regression. This card is NOT done until that is resolved.
+
 ---
 ## (ORIGINAL PROPOSAL ŌĆö kept for context; its kernel-geometry-store mechanism is SUPERSEDED)
